original_thesis: The 'design the rails' stack. Constraints channel behavior; habits automate it; scaffolding supports learning; checklists formalize sequences; cognitive-load theory explains capacity limits; lock-in warns when rails become cages. This stack designs environments where the right behavior is the easy behavior — and knows when the design itself becomes the problem.
what_this_stack_defeats: Willpower-dependent execution, undesigned environments, golden cage effects
notes: Excellent thesis-member alignment. Lock-in as the 'when rails become cages' warning is a sophisticated insight. The duality (design for good behavior vs. awareness of entrapment) is genuinely useful for routing.
